title: Tokenized Real-World Assets Market Hits $51B as Equity Tokenization Surges
summary: The market for tokenized real-world assets (RWAs) has grown 40% to $51 billion, with equity tokenization leading the charge at a 130% increase. Industry players are now racing to define the best business model for this rapidly expanding sector.
body:
The market for tokenized real-world assets (RWAs) has reached a new milestone, surpassing $51 billion in market capitalization. This represents a 40% increase, driven largely by a 130% surge in equity tokenization, according to a report by Bernstein.

The growth highlights the increasing interest in using blockchain technology to represent ownership of traditional assets like stocks, bonds, and real estate. Firms are now competing to establish the most effective business models for tokenizing these assets, aiming to attract both institutional and retail investors.

For everyday investors, this development could mean more accessible and liquid investment opportunities. Tokenized assets can be traded 24/7 on blockchain platforms, potentially offering lower fees and faster settlements compared to traditional markets. However, the industry is still in the early stages, and regulatory clarity remains a key challenge.

As the market evolves, investors should watch for regulatory updates and the emergence of dominant platforms. The race to define the winning business model will likely shape the future of this sector, making it crucial to stay informed about industry developments.

Blockchain tech is making it easier to trade real-world assets like stocks and real estate 24/7. Firms are racing to define the best model for this growing market.

If you're an investor, keep an eye on regulatory updates and emerging platforms. The future of tokenized assets is shaping up fast. Read more → https://www.cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-06-22-tokenized-real-world-assets-market-hits-51b-as-equity-tokenization-surges
